September 13, 1976 
State of California 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Gentlemen: 
Please Reply To: 
P.O. Box 1000 
Huntington Park 
California 90255 
In order to resolve uncertainties which have arisen as to 
whether or not certain conflicts of interest exist, I request 
that the Commission give me opinions in response to the 
three following fact situations: 
1. 
Facts. 
A councilman is one of two shareholders of a 
corporation which has a concession contract with the 
City of South Gate to operate a three-par golf course. 
This contract has approximately two years remaining. 
The corporation receives all receipts from people 
playing the course and pays South Gate a percentage of 
the gross fees. 
The corporation owns equipment for 
taking care of the greens and also owns certain food 
service equipment. This councilman receives in excess 
of $1,000.00 per year from this City. 
Question. 
Does this councilman have a conflict of 
interest in any transactions between the City of South 
Gate and the City of Huntington Park, such as contracts 
for joint maintenance of traffic signals and similar 
activities? 
2. 
Facts. 
The same councilman as in Paragraph 1 above, 
owns 1/4 of the stock of a corporation which has a 
concession for a three-par golf course in Bell Gardens, 
which is owned and operated by the County of Los Angeles. 
This corporation pays a percentage of the revenue from 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
-2-
September 13, 1976 
green fees and other proceeds to the County for this 
concession. 
The corporation owns certain food service 
equipment. 
No net revenue has been received by the 
councilman from this operation, because it is just 
beginning; 
however, it is anticipated that revenue 
will be received within a year. 
Question. 
Does this councilman have a conflict of 
interest in any transactions between the County of Los 
Angeles and the City of Huntington Park, such as contracts 
for road improvement funds, contracts dealing with 
housing and community development projects administered 
by the County, joint use of County-City facilities in 
the Civic Center, contracts with the County for certain 
types of services and similar transactions? 
3. 
Facts. 
I am City Attorney on a part time basis and 
maintain a separate and private office from the office 
provided at the City Hall. 
On occasion in the past, I 
have performed legal services for individual members of 
the City Council, who have individually paid me for 
such services, which have nothing to do with the councilman's 
official duties. 
Question. 
Would it be a conflict of interest for me 
to continue to perform legal services for individual 
councilmen for private legal matters, where I would 
receive more than $250.00 per year for such services? 
Your response to the above questions would be appreciated at 
the earliest opportunity. 
If a different procedure is 
required for the submission of such questions, please advise 
me. 
Yours very truly, 
Elwayne E. Smith 
City Attorney 
EES/dr

EES/dr
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether a city councilman has conflicts of interest regarding: (1) his ownership stake in a golf course concession contract with South Gate, (2) his ownership stake in a golf course concession with Los Angeles County, and (3) whether the city attorney can continue providing private legal services to individual councilmen for fees exceeding $250 per year.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document includes an internal FPPC memorandum at the top summarizing a telephone conversation, followed by the formal written request. OCR quality is moderate with some scanning artifacts. No formal CONCLUSION section exists as this is the incoming inquiry rather than the FPPC's response.

embedding.summary: City Attorney Elwayne E. Smith of Huntington Park requests FPPC opinions on three potential conflict of interest situations involving a city councilman's financial interests in golf course operations and the city attorney's private legal practice with individual councilmembers.
